HalogenGroups / PFASGroups
  • Fingerprint Analysis
  • View page source

Fingerprint Analysis

The ResultsFingerprint class wraps a numpy fingerprint matrix and provides dimensionality reduction, visualisation, and database I/O methods.

Contents

  • ResultsFingerprint

    • Creating a ResultsFingerprint

    • Attributes

  • Dimensionality reduction

    • perform_pca

    • perform_tsne

    • perform_umap

  • Statistical comparison

    • compare_kld

  • Database I/O

    • to_sql / from_sql

    • Summary

ResultsFingerprint

class HalogenGroups.ResultsFingerprint(fingerprints: ndarray, smiles: List[str], group_names: List[str], group_selection: str = 'all', count_mode: str = 'binary', halogens: str | List[str] | None = None, saturation: str | None = None)[source]

Bases: object

Container for PFAS group fingerprints with dimensionality reduction analysis.

This class encapsulates fingerprint representations of HalogenGroups results and provides methods for performing dimensionality reduction (PCA, kernel-PCA, t-SNE, UMAP) and comparison between fingerprint sets using KL divergence.

Parameters:

  • fingerprints (np.ndarray) – Fingerprint matrix (n_molecules, n_groups)

  • smiles (list of str) – SMILES strings for each molecule

  • group_names (list of str) – Names of the PFAS groups in fingerprint

  • group_selection (str) – Type of group selection used

  • count_mode (str) – Encoding mode used (binary, count, max_component)

  • halogens (list of str, optional) – Halogen(s) used for component SMARTS matching. When multiple halogens are given the fingerprint vectors are stacked.

  • saturation (str or None, optional) – Saturation filter applied to component SMARTS groups.

__init__(fingerprints: ndarray, smiles: List[str], group_names: List[str], group_selection: str = 'all', count_mode: str = 'binary', halogens: str | List[str] | None = None, saturation: str | None = None)[source]
summary() → str[source]

Return text summary of fingerprint statistics.

perform_pca(n_components: int = 2, plot: bool = True, output_file: str | None = None) → Dict[str, Any][source]

Perform PCA analysis on fingerprints.

Parameters:

  • n_components (int, default 2) – Number of principal components

  • plot (bool, default True) – Whether to create visualization

  • output_file (str, optional) – Path to save plot (if None, displays interactively)

Returns:

Dictionary containing: - ‘transformed’: PCA-transformed data - ‘explained_variance’: Explained variance ratio - ‘components’: Principal component vectors - ‘pca_model’: Fitted PCA model

Return type:

dict

perform_kernel_pca(n_components: int = 2, kernel: str = 'rbf', gamma: float | None = None, plot: bool = True, output_file: str | None = None) → Dict[str, Any][source]

Perform kernel PCA analysis on fingerprints.

Parameters:

  • n_components (int, default 2) – Number of components

  • kernel (str, default 'rbf') – Kernel type: ‘linear’, ‘poly’, ‘rbf’, ‘sigmoid’, ‘cosine’

  • gamma (float, optional) – Kernel coefficient (if None, uses 1/n_features)

  • plot (bool, default True) – Whether to create visualization

  • output_file (str, optional) – Path to save plot

Returns:

Dictionary containing transformed data and model

Return type:

dict

perform_tsne(n_components: int = 2, perplexity: float = 30.0, learning_rate: float = 200.0, max_iter: int = 1000, plot: bool = True, output_file: str | None = None) → Dict[str, Any][source]

Perform t-SNE analysis on fingerprints.

Parameters:

  • n_components (int, default 2) – Number of dimensions for embedding

  • perplexity (float, default 30.0) – t-SNE perplexity parameter (5-50 typical)

  • learning_rate (float, default 200.0) – Learning rate for optimization

  • max_iter (int, default 1000) – Maximum number of iterations

  • plot (bool, default True) – Whether to create visualization

  • output_file (str, optional) – Path to save plot

Returns:

Dictionary containing transformed data and model

Return type:

dict

perform_umap(n_components: int = 2, n_neighbors: int = 15, min_dist: float = 0.1, metric: str = 'euclidean', plot: bool = True, output_file: str | None = None) → Dict[str, Any][source]

Perform UMAP analysis on fingerprints.

Parameters:

  • n_components (int, default 2) – Number of dimensions for embedding

  • n_neighbors (int, default 15) – Number of neighbors to consider

  • min_dist (float, default 0.1) – Minimum distance between points

  • metric (str, default 'euclidean') – Distance metric

  • plot (bool, default True) – Whether to create visualization

  • output_file (str, optional) – Path to save plot

Returns:

Dictionary containing transformed data and model

Return type:

dict

compare_kld(other: ResultsFingerprint, method: str = 'minmax') → float[source]

Compare two fingerprint sets using KL divergence.

This implements the minmaxKLd method for comparing distributions of PFAS group fingerprints between two datasets.

Parameters:

  • other (ResultsFingerprint) – Other fingerprint set to compare against

  • method (str, default 'minmax') – Comparison method: - ‘minmax’: Min-max normalized KL divergence (symmetric) - ‘forward’: KL(self || other) - ‘reverse’: KL(other || self) - ‘symmetric’: (KL(self || other) + KL(other || self)) / 2

Returns:

KL divergence value (lower = more similar)

Return type:

float

to_sql(conn: str | 'sqlalchemy.engine.Engine' | None = None, filename: str | None = None, table_name: str = 'fingerprints', metadata_table: str = 'fingerprint_metadata', if_exists: str = 'append') → None[source]

Save fingerprints to SQL database.

Parameters:

  • conn (str or SQLAlchemy Engine, optional) – Database connection string or engine

  • filename (str, optional) – SQLite database filename (alternative to conn)

  • table_name (str, default "fingerprints") – Name of the table for fingerprint data

  • metadata_table (str, default "fingerprint_metadata") – Name of the table for metadata

  • if_exists (str, default "append") – How to behave if table exists: ‘fail’, ‘replace’, ‘append’

classmethod from_sql(conn: str | 'sqlalchemy.engine.Engine' | None = None, filename: str | None = None, table_name: str = 'fingerprints', metadata_table: str = 'fingerprint_metadata', limit: int | None = None) → ResultsFingerprint[source]

Load fingerprints from SQL database.

Parameters:

  • conn (str or SQLAlchemy Engine, optional) – Database connection string or engine

  • filename (str, optional) – SQLite database filename

  • table_name (str, default "fingerprints") – Name of the fingerprints table

  • metadata_table (str, default "fingerprint_metadata") – Name of the metadata table

  • limit (int, optional) – Limit number of molecules to load

Returns:

Loaded fingerprints

Return type:

ResultsFingerprint

Creating a ResultsFingerprint

from HalogenGroups import parse_smiles

smiles = ["CCCC(F)(F)F", "FC(F)(F)C(=O)O", "ClCCCl"]
results = parse_smiles(smiles)

fp = results.to_fingerprint()
print(fp.matrix.shape)       # (3, 464)
print(fp.halogens)           # ['F', 'Cl', 'Br', 'I']
print(fp.group_names[0])     # ('perfluoroalkyl', 'F')

Or use generate_fingerprint() directly for a numpy array without the ResultsFingerprint wrapper.

Attributes

Attribute

Description

matrix

numpy.ndarray of shape (n_mols, n_cols)

smiles

List of SMILES (one per row)

group_names

Dict mapping column index → (group_name, halogen) tuple

halogens

Halogens used to generate the fingerprint

saturation

Saturation filter used (None, 'saturated', 'unsaturated')

count_mode

Count mode used ('max_component', 'all', 'binary')

Dimensionality reduction

perform_pca

ResultsFingerprint.perform_pca(n_components: int = 2, plot: bool = True, output_file: str | None = None) → Dict[str, Any][source]

Perform PCA analysis on fingerprints.

Parameters:

  • n_components (int, default 2) – Number of principal components

  • plot (bool, default True) – Whether to create visualization

  • output_file (str, optional) – Path to save plot (if None, displays interactively)

Returns:

Dictionary containing: - ‘transformed’: PCA-transformed data - ‘explained_variance’: Explained variance ratio - ‘components’: Principal component vectors - ‘pca_model’: Fitted PCA model

Return type:

dict

coords = fp.perform_pca(n_components=2, plot=True)
# coords: numpy array (n_mols, 2)

Parameters:

  • n_components (int, default 2): Number of PCA components

  • plot (bool, default False): Generate a scatter plot

  • labels (list, optional): Molecule labels for the plot

  • output (str, optional): File path to save the plot

perform_tsne

ResultsFingerprint.perform_tsne(n_components: int = 2, perplexity: float = 30.0, learning_rate: float = 200.0, max_iter: int = 1000, plot: bool = True, output_file: str | None = None) → Dict[str, Any][source]

Perform t-SNE analysis on fingerprints.

Parameters:

  • n_components (int, default 2) – Number of dimensions for embedding

  • perplexity (float, default 30.0) – t-SNE perplexity parameter (5-50 typical)

  • learning_rate (float, default 200.0) – Learning rate for optimization

  • max_iter (int, default 1000) – Maximum number of iterations

  • plot (bool, default True) – Whether to create visualization

  • output_file (str, optional) – Path to save plot

Returns:

Dictionary containing transformed data and model

Return type:

dict

coords = fp.perform_tsne(n_components=2, perplexity=30, plot=True)

Parameters:

  • n_components (int, default 2)

  • perplexity (float, default 30.0): t-SNE perplexity (typically 5–50)

  • n_iter (int, default 1000): Number of t-SNE iterations

  • plot (bool, default False)

  • labels (list, optional)

Requires: scikit-learn

perform_umap

ResultsFingerprint.perform_umap(n_components: int = 2, n_neighbors: int = 15, min_dist: float = 0.1, metric: str = 'euclidean', plot: bool = True, output_file: str | None = None) → Dict[str, Any][source]

Perform UMAP analysis on fingerprints.

Parameters:

  • n_components (int, default 2) – Number of dimensions for embedding

  • n_neighbors (int, default 15) – Number of neighbors to consider

  • min_dist (float, default 0.1) – Minimum distance between points

  • metric (str, default 'euclidean') – Distance metric

  • plot (bool, default True) – Whether to create visualization

  • output_file (str, optional) – Path to save plot

Returns:

Dictionary containing transformed data and model

Return type:

dict

coords = fp.perform_umap(n_components=2, n_neighbors=15, plot=True)

Parameters:

  • n_components (int, default 2)

  • n_neighbors (int, default 15): UMAP local neighborhood size

  • min_dist (float, default 0.1): Minimum distance between embedded points

  • plot (bool, default False)

Requires: umap-learn (pip install umap-learn)

Statistical comparison

compare_kld

ResultsFingerprint.compare_kld(other: ResultsFingerprint, method: str = 'minmax') → float[source]

Compare two fingerprint sets using KL divergence.

This implements the minmaxKLd method for comparing distributions of PFAS group fingerprints between two datasets.

Parameters:

  • other (ResultsFingerprint) – Other fingerprint set to compare against

  • method (str, default 'minmax') – Comparison method: - ‘minmax’: Min-max normalized KL divergence (symmetric) - ‘forward’: KL(self || other) - ‘reverse’: KL(other || self) - ‘symmetric’: (KL(self || other) + KL(other || self)) / 2

Returns:

KL divergence value (lower = more similar)

Return type:

float

Compute the per-group KL divergence between two fingerprint distributions:

fp_a = parse_smiles(set_a).to_fingerprint()
fp_b = parse_smiles(set_b).to_fingerprint()

kld = fp_a.compare_kld(fp_b, method='minmax')
# kld: dict { group_name: kl_value }

Parameters:

  • other (ResultsFingerprint): The comparison fingerprint

  • method (str, default 'minmax'): - 'minmax': normalise each dimension to [0, 1] before comparison - 'forward': KL(self || other) - 'reverse': KL(other || self) - 'symmetric': average of forward + reverse

Database I/O

to_sql / from_sql

ResultsFingerprint.to_sql(conn: str | 'sqlalchemy.engine.Engine' | None = None, filename: str | None = None, table_name: str = 'fingerprints', metadata_table: str = 'fingerprint_metadata', if_exists: str = 'append') → None[source]

Save fingerprints to SQL database.

Parameters:

  • conn (str or SQLAlchemy Engine, optional) – Database connection string or engine

  • filename (str, optional) – SQLite database filename (alternative to conn)

  • table_name (str, default "fingerprints") – Name of the table for fingerprint data

  • metadata_table (str, default "fingerprint_metadata") – Name of the table for metadata

  • if_exists (str, default "append") – How to behave if table exists: ‘fail’, ‘replace’, ‘append’

classmethod ResultsFingerprint.from_sql(conn: str | 'sqlalchemy.engine.Engine' | None = None, filename: str | None = None, table_name: str = 'fingerprints', metadata_table: str = 'fingerprint_metadata', limit: int | None = None) → ResultsFingerprint[source]

Load fingerprints from SQL database.

Parameters:

  • conn (str or SQLAlchemy Engine, optional) – Database connection string or engine

  • filename (str, optional) – SQLite database filename

  • table_name (str, default "fingerprints") – Name of the fingerprints table

  • metadata_table (str, default "fingerprint_metadata") – Name of the metadata table

  • limit (int, optional) – Limit number of molecules to load

Returns:

Loaded fingerprints

Return type:

ResultsFingerprint

# Save
fp.to_sql("fingerprints.db")

# Load
from HalogenGroups import ResultsFingerprint
fp2 = ResultsFingerprint.from_sql("fingerprints.db")
print(fp2.matrix.shape)

Matrices are stored in sparse format for efficient disk usage. Metadata (halogens, saturation, count_mode, group_names) is preserved.

Summary

ResultsFingerprint.summary() → str[source]

Return text summary of fingerprint statistics.

Print a human-readable summary of the fingerprint:

fp.summary()
# ResultsFingerprint
# ------------------
# Molecules : 3
# Halogens  : ['F', 'Cl', 'Br', 'I']
# Saturation: None
# Shape     : (3, 464)
# Non-zero  : 4 cells
